sections.heading: As an existing Atlas account holder, how do I create a new Atlas project?
sections.body:
To learn how to create a project, see
[create a project](create-project).

sections.heading: As an existing MongoDB Cloud Manager account holder, how do I create a new MongoDB Atlas project?
sections.body:
When creating a new Project, Cloud Manager offers you the choice to create a
Cloud Manager project or an Atlas project.

To learn how to create a project, see
[create a project](create-project).

sections.heading: How do I restore lost access to organizations and projects?
sections.body:
If you created your Atlas account using a Gmail or Google Apps
account, the following can cause you to lose access to your
organizations and projects:

- The Google email address with which you signed up differs from the
  email address associated to your account. This is most often the case
  with Gmail or Google Apps aliases.

- Your username or email domain has changed in Google Apps.

To re-gain access to your organizations and projects:

- Ask an Atlas user with the [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) to
  invite you to the organization to which you lost access, or

- Contact MongoDB Support.

sections.heading: How do I remove my project?
sections.body:
You can remove a project if:

- You have the [Project Owner](<Project Owner>) role for the project.

- The project has no outstanding invoices.

- The project has no active clusters.

To delete a project for an organization, you can delete from the
organization's **Projects** view or the project's
**Project Setting** view. For details, see
[Delete a Project](delete-project).

sections.heading: How do I delete my Atlas account?
sections.body:
To delete your Atlas account, see [delete](delete-atlas-account).

> **Note:** Atlas requires up to two weeks to fully process an account deletion.
> Until then, you can't create a new Atlas account with the email address
> associated with your deleted account.

To learn more, see Request Support.
